toc.title: ColecoVision Technical Notes
toc.description: PRIMARY SYSTEM QUICK REFERENCE. Old online ColecoVision technical note covering cartridge headers at $8000, BIOS title-screen behavior, title string format, built-in title character set, controller mode toggles ($80 keypad/right buttons and $C0 joystick/left buttons), keypad return codes, joystick bits, memory map (BIOS, expansion, RAM mirror, cartridge ROM), I/O decode ranges, SN76489 sound overview, and TMS9918A video basics. Use first for quick system, cartridge, controller, memory-map, and I/O-map questions.

toc.title: TMS9918A VDP - Sean Young
toc.description: PRIMARY VDP REFERENCE. Sean Young's detailed TMS9918A documentation, useful for ColecoVision VDP behavior. Covers colors, registers R0-R7, status register bits, external video, VDP memory access, command/register access quirks, interrupt behavior, all official display modes (Graphic I, Text, Graphic II, Multicolor), undocumented mode combinations, sprite attributes, sprite sizes, illegal sprites, collision behavior, fifth-sprite flag, 4 KB/16 KB VRAM addressing, pinouts, and references. Use first for low-level VDP emulation and graphics debugging.

toc.title: SN76489A PSG - ColecoVision Sound Generation Hardware
toc.description: PRIMARY COLECOVISION PSG QUICK REFERENCE. Concise SN76489A programming note for the ColecoVision sound chip. Covers the three tone generators, 10-bit frequency period format, frequency formula f = 3.579 MHz / (32n), attenuation bit weights and silence value, periodic versus white noise feedback, noise shift-rate modes including tone generator 3 source, register address fields, latch/data byte formats, and attenuator/noise control words. Use for SN76489 register writes and sound-emulation questions.

toc.title: Super Game Module Technical Notes - Opcode
toc.description: PRIMARY SGM MEMORY REFERENCE. Opcode Super Game Module technical notes covering the 24 KB upper RAM mapping at $2000-$7FFF, optional 8 KB lower RAM mapping over BIOS at $0000-$1FFF, ADAM compatibility requirements, detection/initialization sequence, port $53 RAM enable, port $7F BIOS/RAM mapping values, warnings about ADAM memory conflicts, and SGM AY-3-8910 sound mapping summary. Use first for SGM RAM, ADAM compatibility, and SGM initialization/debugging questions.
